what_it_does: Records a raised signal that the operator has decided the visual-fidelity issue by choosing the option 'Push the drawn style further first', and states that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity may resume.
observations: The item type is 'signal' with source 'claude_code' and state 'raised'.
Created and updated timestamps are identical.
The body explicitly names the chosen option and notes that the resume itself is the lane's/operator's gated step.
standouts: Explicit chosen option: 'Push the drawn style further first'.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
History records a single state transition from null to raised.
Markdown body restates and clarifies the decision signal.
notes: A structured decision signal announcing the selected visual-fidelity option and telling gated work it can resume.
questions: What specific work is gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
What does 'drawn style' refer to in this project context?
Who or what performs the actual resume step?
How was this option selected over alternatives?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a decision signal that records the chosen option and communicates resumption.
intention_signals: Broadcast a resolved design direction so downstream tasks waiting on the decision can proceed.
contribution: Captures a human decision and its downstream resume implication in a structured, addressable signal that a fused system can use to unblock gated work.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal recording that the operator decided visual-fidelity by choosing 'Push the drawn style further first' and indicating gated work may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To communicate a resolved option and authorize downstream work gated on the visual-fidelity decision to proceed.
